human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for food-and-beverage manufacturing specificity, source quality, hold and traceability boundary discipline, supplier escalation posture, commercialization completeness, and explicit specification-based runtime positioning.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to food-and-beverage quality evidence review, traceability conflict analysis, hold and release-boundary handling, supplier-quality escalation, and corrective-action packaging inside food manufacturing.

Authority sources cover FDA food-manufacturing, traceability, supplier, and recall references plus SQFI, Codex, and GS1 standards that materially change terminology and evidence expectations for this lane.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, repeat defects, control-bypass requests, traceability conflicts, supplier substitution pressure, low-confidence handling, recall-authority escalation, tenant-specific adaptation, and attempts to close findings without review support.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-and-beverage-manufacturing/quality-operations-food-and-beverage-specialist/ so the lane stays distinct from adjacent quality, food-safety, production planning, procurement, inventory, and logistics ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final release, recall command, supplier substitution authority, and unsupported hold overrides.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated food-and-beverage manufacturing quality-operations specialist that supports evidence-backed hold, release-boundary, nonconformance, supplier, and traceability workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately approved retained implementation is promoted.
